---
title: "Well-Being Analysis — Dashboard"
format:
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.facecolor": "white",
    "axes.facecolor": "#FAFAFA",
    "axes.grid": True,
    "axes.axisbelow": True,
    "grid.color": "#E8E8E8",
    "grid.linestyle": "--",
    "axes.titlesize": 11,
    "axes.titleweight": "bold",
    "axes.labelsize": 10,
    "xtick.labelsize": 8,
    "ytick.labelsize": 8,
})

_ID = {"person_id", "employee_id"}

def numeric_features(df, target):
    num = df.select_dtypes(include="number").columns.tolist()
    return [c for c in num if c != target and c not in _ID]

def target_correlations(df, target):
    feats = numeric_features(df, target)
    return df[feats + [target]].corr(numeric_only=True)[target].drop(target)

def run_pca(df, target):
    feats = numeric_features(df, target)
    X = df[feats].dropna()
    X_scaled = StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)
    pca = PCA()
    scores = pca.fit_transform(X_scaled)
    return scores, pca.explained_variance_ratio_

def plot_corr(df, target, title):
    corr = target_correlations(df, target).sort_values()
    colors = ["#B65E5A" if v < 0 else "#4C78A8" for v in corr.values]
    h = max(3, len(corr) * 0.4)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, h))
    ax.barh(corr.index, corr.values, color=colors, edgecolor="none")
    ax.axvline(0, color="#444", linewidth=1)
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.set_xlabel("Pearson r")
    for i, v in enumerate(corr.values):
        ha = "left" if v >= 0 else "right"
        ax.text(v + (0.01 if v >= 0 else -0.01), i, f"{v:.2f}",
                va="center", ha=ha, fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    return fig

def plot_var(var_ratio, title):
    pcs = np.arange(1, len(var_ratio) + 1)
    cum = np.cumsum(var_ratio)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
    bars = ax.bar(pcs, var_ratio, color="#4C78A8", width=0.7, edgecolor="none",
                  label="Per-component")
    ax.plot(pcs, cum, marker="o", color="#222", linewidth=2, label="Cumulative")
    ax.set_xticks(pcs)
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.set_xlabel("PC")
    ax.set_ylabel("Variance ratio")
    for rect, v in zip(bars, var_ratio):
        ax.text(rect.get_x() + rect.get_width() / 2,
                rect.get_height() + 0.01, f"{v:.2f}", ha="center", fontsize=8)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    return fig

DATA_DIR = "data/clean_data"
df_pca      = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_pca.csv")
df_prod     = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_productivity.csv")
df_sleep    = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_sleep.csv")
df_students = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_students.csv")
df_remote   = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_remote.csv")

# Compute key stats for value boxes
total_obs = len(df_pca) + len(df_prod) + len(df_sleep) + len(df_students) + len(df_remote)

all_corrs = []
for df, tgt in [
    (df_pca, "wellness"),
    (df_prod, "productivity"),
    (df_sleep, "stress_level"),
    (df_students, "stress_numeric"),
    (df_remote, "productivity_score"),
]:
    corr = target_correlations(df, tgt)
    best_feat = corr.abs().idxmax()
    all_corrs.append((abs(corr[best_feat]), f"{best_feat} = {corr[best_feat]:.2f}"))

best_corr_val, best_corr_label = max(all_corrs, key=lambda x: x[0])
```

## Key Metrics

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Datasets Analysed"
dict(icon="database", color="info", value=5)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Observations"
dict(icon="people-fill", color="success", value=f"{total_obs:,}")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Strongest Single Correlation"
dict(icon="graph-up-arrow", color="warning", value=best_corr_label)
```

## Correlation with Target {.tabset}

### Lifestyle

```{python}
fig = plot_corr(df_pca, "wellness", "Lifestyle — Correlation with Wellness")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Productivity

```{python}
fig = plot_corr(df_prod, "productivity", "Productivity — Correlation with Productivity")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Sleep

```{python}
fig = plot_corr(df_sleep, "stress_level", "Sleep — Correlation with Stress Level")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Students

```{python}
fig = plot_corr(df_students, "stress_numeric", "Students — Correlation with Stress")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Remote Work

```{python}
fig = plot_corr(df_remote, "productivity_score", "Remote Work — Correlation with Productivity Score")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

## PCA Explained Variance {.tabset}

### Lifestyle

```{python}
_, vr = run_pca(df_pca, "wellness")
fig = plot_var(vr, "Lifestyle — PCA Explained Variance")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Productivity

```{python}
_, vr = run_pca(df_prod, "productivity")
fig = plot_var(vr, "Productivity — PCA Explained Variance")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Sleep

```{python}
_, vr = run_pca(df_sleep, "stress_level")
fig = plot_var(vr, "Sleep — PCA Explained Variance")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Students

```{python}
_, vr = run_pca(df_students, "stress_numeric")
fig = plot_var(vr, "Students — PCA Explained Variance")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```

### Remote Work

```{python}
_, vr = run_pca(df_remote, "productivity_score")
fig = plot_var(vr, "Remote Work — PCA Explained Variance")
plt.show()
plt.close(fig)
```
